headline: ExxonMobil Board Unanimously Recommends Redomiciling the Company from New Jersey to Texas
summary: SPRING, Texas, March 10, 2026--Exxon Mobil Corporation today announced its Board of Directors has unanimously recommended shareholders approve changing the company’s legal domicile from New Jersey to Texas. The Board concluded that aligning ExxonMobil’s legal domicile with where its leadership and core operations have been based since 1989 will benefit shareholders.

content.title: ExxonMobil Board Unanimously Recommends Redomiciling the Company from New Jersey to Texas
content.summary: SPRING, Texas, March 10, 2026--Exxon Mobil Corporation today announced its Board of Directors has unanimously recommended shareholders approve changing the company’s legal domicile from New Jersey to Texas. The Board concluded that aligning ExxonMobil’s legal domicile with where its leadership and core operations have been based since 1989 will benefit shareholders.

headline: Exxon Eyes Texas for Legal Home After 144 Years in New Jersey
summary: In a proxy filing on Tuesday, the oil company said moving would mean operating in a more favorable business environment where officials are more familiar with its operations, provide greater certainty for decision-making, and align its legal and physical headquarters.  Exxon asked investors to vote in favor of the transfer in its annual shareholder meeting in May.  Domiciling in Texas “may reduce the risk of future frivolous litigation against the Texas Corporation and its directors and officers,” Exxon said in the filing.

content.title: Exxon Eyes Texas for Legal Home After 144 Years in New Jersey

Domiciling in Texas “may reduce the risk of future frivolous litigation against the Texas Corporation and its directors and officers,” Exxon said in the filing.

headline: Exxon Eyes Texas as Legal Home After 144 Years in New Jersey
summary: In a proxy filing on Tuesday, North America’s biggest oil company said moving would mean operating in a more favorable business environment where officials are more familiar with its operations, provide greater certainty for decision-making, and align its legal and physical headquarters.  Exxon asked investors to vote in favor of the transfer in its annual shareholder meeting in May.  Domiciling in Texas “may reduce the risk of future frivolous litigation against the Texas Corporation and its directors and officers,” Exxon, which already is headquartered in the state, said in the filing.

content.title: Exxon Eyes Texas as Legal Home After 144 Years in New Jersey
content.summary: In a proxy filing on Tuesday, North America’s biggest oil company said moving would mean operating in a more favorable business environment where officials are more familiar with its operations, provide greater certainty for decision-making, and align its legal and physical headquarters.  Exxon asked investors to vote in favor of the transfer in its annual shareholder meeting in May.  Domiciling in Texas “may reduce the risk of future frivolous litigation against the Texas Corporation and its directors and officers,” Exxon, which already is headquartered in the state, said in the filing.

headline: Amazon Looks to Raise at Least $37 Billion Through Bond Sale
summary: The tech giant is targeting the equivalent of about $37 billion to $42 billion in a cross-Atlantic offering in dollars and euros, according to people with knowledge of the matter.  The firm is marketing US high-grade debt in as many as 11 tranches, ranging from two to 50 years, and it seeks to raise $25 billion to $30 billion, the people said, asking not to be identified because discussions are private.  Initial price discussions for the longest portion of that offering — a note maturing in 2076 — involve a yield of about 1.55 percentage points more than Treasuries, one of the people said.

content.title: Amazon Looks to Raise at Least $37 Billion Through Bond Sale
content.summary: The tech giant is targeting the equivalent of about $37 billion to $42 billion in a cross-Atlantic offering in dollars and euros, according to people with knowledge of the matter.  The firm is marketing US high-grade debt in as many as 11 tranches, ranging from two to 50 years, and it seeks to raise $25 billion to $30 billion, the people said, asking not to be identified because discussions are private.  Initial price discussions for the longest portion of that offering — a note maturing in 2076 — involve a yield of about 1.55 percentage points more than Treasuries, one of the people said.

headline: Exxon Mobil Evacuates Some Workers From Middle East
summary: Exxon Mobil's nonessential staff has been evacuated from the Middle East, according to a source familiar with the matter.  The company has nonessential staff in Qatar and the United Arab Emirates, where it is a partner in oil fields and liquified-natural-gas ventures.  Exxon also has workers in Saudi Arabia, where it has five joint ventures with Saudi Aramco and the country's chemical company to refine oil into fuel and make petrochemicals.
